Operation 'executeGraphQL' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description is too brief and lacks critical details. Enhanced version explains what inputs are expected (GraphQL request structure), what is returned (data or errors), authentication requirements, and key capabilities (subscriptions). This helps AI agents understand when to use this endpoint and what to expect.

data.current_description: Main GraphQL endpoint for queries, mutations, and subscriptions
data.description_suggestion: Execute GraphQL queries, mutations, or subscriptions against your data. Accepts a GraphQL request containing the operation, variables, and operation name. Returns a GraphQL response with data or errors. Requires admin secret authentication. Supports real-time subscriptions for live data updates.
data.reasoning: Current description is too brief and lacks critical details. Enhanced version explains what inputs are expected (GraphQL request structure), what is returned (data or errors), authentication requirements, and key capabilities (subscriptions). This helps AI agents understand when to use this endpoint and what to expect.

message: Operation 'executeMetadata' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks specificity about what metadata operations are supported and their effects. Enhanced version clarifies the types of operations (tables, relationships, permissions, triggers), input/output structure, state-changing nature, and persistence behavior. This enables AI agents to understand the scope and impact of metadata operations.

data.current_description: Metadata API for managing Hasura configuration
data.description_suggestion: Execute metadata operations to manage Hasura configuration including tables, relationships, permissions, and event triggers. Accepts a MetadataRequest containing the operation type and parameters. Returns operation results or errors. Modifies Hasura's internal metadata state. Requires admin secret authentication. Changes are persisted to the metadata database.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about what metadata operations are supported and their effects. Enhanced version clarifies the types of operations (tables, relationships, permissions, triggers), input/output structure, state-changing nature, and persistence behavior. This enables AI agents to understand the scope and impact of metadata operations.

message: Operation 'executeQuery' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, not_actionable. Current description is too minimal and doesn't clearly communicate deprecation status or migration path. Enhanced version explicitly states deprecation, recommends alternatives based on use case, and clarifies backward compatibility. This prevents AI agents from using deprecated endpoints and guides them to appropriate alternatives.

data.description_suggestion: Legacy query endpoint for database operations. This endpoint is deprecated and should not be used for new implementations. Use /v1/metadata instead for metadata operations or /v1/graphql for GraphQL queries. Maintained for backward compatibility only.
data.reasoning: Current description is too minimal and doesn't clearly communicate deprecation status or migration path. message: Operation 'healthCheck' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_context. Current description lacks details about response format, HTTP status codes, and use cases. Enhanced version clarifies the success response, typical use cases (monitoring, load balancing, readiness checks), and authentication requirements. This helps AI agents understand the endpoint's purpose in system monitoring and when to invoke it.

data.current_description: Returns health status of the Hasura instance
data.description_suggestion: Check the health status of the Hasura GraphQL Engine instance. Returns HTTP 200 with health status if the instance is operational. Use this endpoint for monitoring, load balancer health checks, and readiness verification. No authentication required.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks details about response format, HTTP status codes, and use cases. Enhanced version clarifies the success response, typical use cases (monitoring, load balancing, readiness checks), and authentication requirements. This helps AI agents understand the endpoint's purpose in system monitoring and when to invoke it.

message: Operation 'executeQueryV2' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Current description is vague about capabilities and lacks input/output details. Enhanced version specifies supported operations (run_sql), clarifies the request/response structure, authentication requirements, and use cases (advanced database access). This enables AI agents to understand when to use this endpoint versus GraphQL for database operations.

data.current_description: Query endpoint for database operations like run_sql
data.description_suggestion: Execute database operations including raw SQL queries via run_sql and other database-level commands. Accepts a query request with the operation type and parameters. Returns operation results or database errors. Requires admin secret authentication. Supports direct database access for advanced operations not available through GraphQL.
data.reasoning: Current description is vague about capabilities and lacks input/output details. This enables AI agents to understand when to use this endpoint versus GraphQL for database operations.
